skill.name: image-outpainting
skill.description: Image outpainting on RunComfy via the runcomfy CLI — extend a still beyond its original canvas, fill in what the camera did not capture, change aspect ratio while preserving the original content. Routes across Nano Banana 2 Edit, GPT Image 2 Edit, FLUX Kontext Pro, and brand edit endpoints.
skill.summary: Extend images beyond their canvas with AI-powered outpainting via the RunComfy CLI.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: All 88 static findings are false positives. The 51 external_commands are bash code-block examples invoking the runcomfy CLI, scoped to Bash(runcomfy *) via the skill's allowed-tools field. The 23 network findings are documentation URLs to runcomfy.com, docs.runcomfy.com, and skills.sh — all explicitly allowlisted. The 2 filesystem findings reference ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json storage documentation (not actual access). The 12 weak-crypto and 3 system-reconnaissance findings are false positives from pattern matching on text like camera distance and token storage. The skill includes a transparent Security & Privacy section documenting shell-injection mitigations, indirect prompt injection warnings, and an outbound endpoint allowlist.

content.user_title: Extend Images Beyond Their Canvas with AI Outpainting
content.value_statement: Cropped photos and fixed aspect ratios limit how images can be used across platforms. This skill extends stills beyond their original canvas, uncrops portraits, and changes aspect ratios while preserving the original content using the runcomfy CLI.
actual_capabilities: Extend images beyond their original canvas in any direction (left, right, top, bottom)
Change aspect ratios (square to 16:9, portrait to landscape) while preserving original content
Uncrop portraits to reveal more of the subject and surrounding environment
Route across multiple AI models including Nano Banana 2 Edit, GPT Image 2 Edit, and FLUX Kontext Pro
Maintain brand-locked continuation using Seedream, Dreamina, Qwen, or FLUX 2 edit endpoints
Chain multi-step outpaint passes to extend images incrementally by 30-50% per pass
limitations: Does not support mask-driven local edits (use the image-inpainting skill instead)
Does not extend video canvases spatially (use the video-outpainting skill for that)
CLI cannot reach ComfyUI GUI workflows; these require opening in RunComfy ComfyUI cloud
Quality may degrade at extension boundaries with rapid chained passes beyond 2-3 iterations

use_cases.title: Social Media Aspect-Ratio Adaptation
use_cases.description: Convert square product photos to 9:16 for Instagram Reels or 16:9 for YouTube thumbnails without losing the original subject.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: E-commerce Product Photography
use_cases.description: Uncrop tight product shots for catalog pages, adding matching environment and lighting context around the product.

use_cases.title: Historical Photo Restoration
use_cases.description: Restore cropped historical photographs by extending the canvas with period-appropriate reference images.
use_cases.target_user: Archivists, historians, and photo restoration specialists

prompt_templates.title: Basic Aspect-Ratio Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Outpaint this image from 1:1 to 16:9 aspect ratio. Add matching environment on the left and right sides. Keep the original subject exactly as it is.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple canvas extension for a square photo that needs to fit a widescreen layout

prompt_templates.title: Uncrop a Portrait
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend the canvas downward to show the subject's full upper body and arms. Continue the existing clothing style, lighting, and background. Keep the face and current visible area exactly as in the input.
prompt_templates.scenario: Tight head-and-shoulders shot needs more body visible for a professional headshot layout

prompt_templates.title: Reference-Style Outpaint
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend this image to 21:9 cinematic aspect ratio. Use the reference image's environment style (lighting, color palette, texture) for the new canvas areas. Preserve the original subject's identity, pose, and framing exactly.
prompt_templates.scenario: When the base model produces visible seams, provide a reference image to match the extension style

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Step Chain Extension
prompt_templates.prompt: Pass 1: Extend the canvas to the right by ~40%, adding matching landscape. Pass 2: Use the pass-1 output as input and extend further right by another ~30%. Each pass preserves the cumulative original content.
prompt_templates.scenario: When a single outpaint pass cannot achieve the desired final canvas size

output_examples.input: A square product photo of a coffee mug on a wooden table, tightly cropped
output_examples.output: A 16:9 widescreen image with the mug centered, wooden table surface extended left and right, matching lighting and grain texture, original mug unchanged

output_examples.input: A head-and-shoulders portrait with a blurred office background
output_examples.output: A portrait with extended canvas showing the subject's torso and arms, the office background continued naturally with matching depth-of-field blur, face and expression preserved exactly

output_examples.input: A cropped historical family photo from the 1950s
output_examples.output: A restored photo with extended canvas showing the full room context, period-appropriate furniture and decor continued from a reference photo, original subjects preserved with identity intact

best_practices: Lead prompts with the canvas change direction (extend left, extend downward, change to 16:9) before describing the extension content
Always end prompts with explicit preservation language like 'Keep the original subject exactly as in the input' to prevent subtle regeneration of the original area
Set aspect_ratio explicitly in the JSON input rather than relying on the model to infer it from the prompt alone
anti_patterns: Piping an arbitrary remote install script into a shell; always install the runcomfy CLI via npm or npx from the verified package
Using this skill for mask-driven internal edits (filling holes inside the canvas) when the image-inpainting skill is the correct tool
Chaining more than 3 outpaint passes in a single session, which causes quality degradation at extension boundaries

faq.question: What is the difference between outpainting and inpainting?
faq.answer: Outpainting extends the canvas beyond the original image boundaries, adding new content around the edges. Inpainting fills in missing or masked areas inside the existing canvas. This skill handles outpainting only.

faq.question: Which model should I use for simple aspect-ratio changes?
faq.answer: Use Nano Banana 2 Edit as the default for prompt-shaped outpainting. It handles spatial language well and preserves the original content. For complex cases with visible seams, switch to GPT Image 2 Edit with a reference image or FLUX Kontext Pro for maximum preservation.

faq.question: Can I chain multiple outpaint passes?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the output of pass 1 as the input for pass 2. Keep each pass to 30-50% extension to avoid quality degradation at the boundary seams. More than 3 passes typically shows visible artifacts.

faq.question: Is the runcomfy CLI safe to install?
faq.answer: Install only via the verified npm package: npm i -g @runcomfy/cli or npx -y @runcomfy/cli. Never pipe a remote install script into a shell. The CLI stores the API token in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600.

faq.question: Does this skill work with video?
faq.answer: No. This skill is for still images only. For extending the canvas of video frames, use the video-outpainting skill instead.

faq.question: What are the CLI exit codes?
faq.answer: 0 = success, 64 = bad CLI args, 65 = bad input JSON or schema mismatch, 69 = upstream 5xx error, 75 = retryable timeout or rate limit (429), 77 = not signed in or token rejected.
